Peter,

Here at EMILY's List, we're focused on one path forward in 2018: electing more pro-choice Democratic women.

It starts with flipping the House and Senate.

But we also need to elect more pro-choice Democratic women governors and state legislators. _And ___ we're focusing on elected offices that don't always make the news but have a big impact, like secretaries of state who oversee our elections.

Supporting so many women means we all need to do our part. So this January Membership Week, I'm reviewing our supporter records, and here's what they show for this email address:
**2018 Member:** Not yet
**Suggested action:[Become a member for 2018](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6b73/24c69fd9/135e71c05/18926eb1/418259048/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180112_email_JanMembershipWeek?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+Become+a+member+for+2018&utm_campaign=WQEFRM27N_digital_email_20180117_JanuarySupporterRecord_inact&source=WQEFRM27N_digital_email_20180117_JanuarySupporterRecord_inact")**
The best way to support the dozens -- potentially hundreds -- of candidates relying on us is to become a member for 2018.

You will be signaling to all of our women who are running that their fight is our fight. And you'll get the card to prove it, too.

**Donate $3 to become a member for 2018 and show our candidates that you have their back:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/January-Membership-Week](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6b73/24c69fd9/135e71c05/18926eb2/418259048/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20180112_email_JanMembershipWeek?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgJanuaryMembershi&utm_campaign=WQEFRM27N_digital_email_20180117_JanuarySupporterRecord_inact&source=WQEFRM27N_digital_email_20180117_JanuarySupporterRecord_inact")**

Thanks -- and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List

name: The White House
subject: A promise kept
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0bcd726d904627de44e90ec5f8354dc079365858118f7592493adde398b56105e10d98b3eb5a1b9e471050e41241ed1931a5793dddc5ad4c

A promise kept

Three months ago, President Donald J. Trump spoke before men and women of the American Trucking Associations in Harrisburg, Pennsylvania. At the time, while he had good news to report on the economy, Congress was still working to send tax cuts to his desk.

“We want lower taxes, bigger paychecks, and more jobs for American truckers and for American workers,” the President said.

Today, he returns to Pennsylvania—with even better news to share. Less than a month after the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act became law, at least 187 companies have issued bonuses, raised their minimum wages, planned investments, or donated to charity as a result of lower taxes.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0bcd726d904627de276cc05ceaf62b784208ed2b4d8aefc9adcbc48cab920460d9925a46253771d5a0522aff38a1f66c552f70a0ff279433
See a list of those companies here.

Overlooked in the immigration debate

The Departments of Justice and Homeland Security released a report this week showing that 3 out of 4 individuals convicted of international terrorism-related charges between September 11, 2001, and December 31, 2016, are foreign-born individuals who entered the United States through our immigration system.

Recent reports from DOJ and DHS also draw attention to an issue often lost in the immigration debate: gender-based violence.

- A 2011 Government Accountability Office report found that criminal aliens were convicted of 69,929 sex offenses between fiscal years 2003 and 2009.
- A 2014 study estimated that approximately 1,500 forced marriages occur in the U.S. every year.
- That same 2014 study showed there to be an average of 23 to 27 honor killings in America each year—with 90 percent of victims murdered for being “too westernized.”

Numbers like these show that regardless of political affiliation, all Americans should want an immigration system that supports and defends our values.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0bcd726d904627de6e3ab8de96d30c9503ee339980347d619359e9733166521ea47a6e674d7814b9f820a6b4e055c8b61101e966c51169e6
Read more about how our current immigration system jeopardizes American security.

‘Why taxes matter after all’

Apple announced yesterday a “sweeping set of moves partially tied to the recent tax bill” that will see the company pay $38 billion in taxes from overseas profits while creating 20,000 jobs in the United States, USA Today reports.

“Apple is a success story that could only have happened in America, and we are proud to build on our long history of support for the U.S. economy,” Apple CEO Tim Cook said.

The news is significant, and Stephen Moore—a columnist for The Washington Times—explains why. “One premise of modern-day ‘progressives,’ is that taxes don’t have much influence on how much and when people invest, how much they work and save, or where they live,” Moore writes.

“Boy, has the just-enacted Trump tax bill already proved these critics wrong.”

Read more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0bcd726d904627de09048fec3e78d4b493ddab1efad6e9feca8f22ef9a37b8434976392663815aeaa2ca9153a1e1f2b6b73a1bec06ff8419
’Why taxes matter after all’

Photo of the Day

Congressional Gold Medal recipient Senator Bob Dole | January 17, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

This morning, President Trump and Vice President Pence will meet with Secretary of State Rex Tillerson, followed by a meeting with Senior Military Leadership at the Pentagon.

In the afternoon, the President will take a tour and give remarks at H&K Equipment Company in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania.
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0bcd726d904627de315214d68890db53f3c76c4827f12e98a848be0911f395c083fc2f24265a9b7f76c4217d04666ead3a491fb823731777
Watch live at 3:05pm ET.

The Vice President and Second Lady will host a roundtable discussion with Students for Life and then deliver remarks at a reception honoring pro-life supporters, leaders, and participants of the annual March for Life event.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0bcd726d904627dec79528b9e8ad2d42b49ceb6a9abdb3e3ab44aa9097d89c285a15946149585b44f20d293c2ddca8ea45be99b22c1167fc
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=0bcd726d904627deb0a414dcff2a988b53214628535c7157caa3a8cc9f5b841c742e47d74eb6f0e4c71b6a8dbd4878908529ae5fc4e015bd
Contact the White House |

Unsubscribe

subject: Don't Let GOP Stop Mueller's Investigation of Donald Trump
body:
Republicans want to end the investigation into Trump’s Russia ties. We cannot let them. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Congress_Investigate_Trump_dems)

The petition to congressional leadership reads:
“The congressional investigation into Trump cannot conclude until Special Prosecutor Mueller has wrapped up his investigation.”

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Congress_Investigate_Trump_dems)

Dear Activist,

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Congress_Investigate_Trump_dems)

Republicans have sought every chance to delegitimize special counsel Robert Mueller. Now, they are ramping up their attempts to derail Mueller’s inquiry into Trump’s Russia ties with calls to shut down the parallel, ongoing congressional investigations, thus making it appear as if Mueller is on a wild goose chase.1

Republicans have spent months stymying the investigation by refusing to schedule interviews with key witnesses and sitting on requests from Democrats to subpoena relevant documents. Now powerful House Republican leaders such as Rep. Trey Gowdy – an extreme right-wing Republican ideologue – are reportedly talking about shutting down the investigation.2 This is unacceptable.

Trump’s lapdogs in Congress are making up excuses to conclude their investigation into Trump’s Russia ties to pressure Mueller to end his investigation – and shut down Democrats like Rep. Adam Schiff who have relentlessly pursued the truth. We need a large outcry right now to shame Republicans into continuing their investigation.

Tell congressional leaders: Don’t bow to Trump by ending the Russia investigation. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Congress_Investigate_Trump_dems)

There is no way congressional investigators have actually finished their work. No one yet knows the full extent of Trump’s ties to Russia, and special counsel Mueller keeps uncovering new information that warrants congressional investigation.

Republicans are sabotaging an investigation with national security implications. House Intelligence Committee Chair Devin Nunes repeatedly refused requests from his Democratic colleagues for interviews with as many as 30 witnesses and blocked potential subpoenas of relevant documents.3 Financial crimes are notoriously difficult to uncover, and thorough investigations can take years.4

It is evident the investigation to date has not been sufficiently thorough. Democratic Congressman Jim Himes says that there are so many unanswered questions that Democrats would have to list them in a minority report if Republicans get their way. 5

Mueller and the congressional investigators have two different jobs. Mueller’s narrow focus is on whether anyone connected to the Trump organization – or Trump himself – committed specific crimes by coordinating with Russians. Congress is investigating Russia’s attempt to hijack our democracy and whether Trump or his campaign engaged in shameful and improper collusion with Russia even if it does not violate specific laws. Congress’ responsibility is to ensure thorough fact finding to uncover answers for the American public. Republicans are refusing to get the real facts in a bid to undermine Robert Mueller, and we cannot let that happen.

Tell congressional leaders: Don’t bow to Trump by ending the Russia investigation. Click here to sign the petition. (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Congress_Investigate_Trump_dems)

The effort to derail Mueller began among fringe supporters and the alt-right fake news, but Trump Republicans are now blatantly launching a systematic and coordinated attack on his investigation. The current Republican attempts to end the ongoing congressional investigations are part of an alliance with the extreme right-wing and Trump sycophants in Congress to systematically undermine Mueller and his investigation.6 By insisting that there are no other questions to ask, or evidence to examine, Republicans hope to create a media firestorm from the extreme right wing that will either shut down or sabotage Mueller’s investigation.

The public has a right to know the extent of the potential collusion between Trump and his associates and the Kremlin. As long as there are witnesses to interview, and questions remain, Congress must keep up this crucial work.

Tell congressional leaders: Don’t bow to Trump by ending Russia investigation. Click the link below to sign the petition.
https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Congress_Investigate_Trump_dems (https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Congress_Investigate_Trump_dems)

Thank you for speaking out,

Kaili Lambe, Director of Organizing
CREDO Action from Working Assets (http://www.credoaction.com)

Add your name:

(https://act.credoaction.com/sign/Congress_Investigate_Trump_dems)
References:

Nick Baumann and Ryan J. Reilly, “Republicans Have Dramatically Ramped Up Their Attacks On Russia Probes In 2018 (https://www.huffingtonpost.com/entry/donald-trump-robert-mueller-investigations-gop_us_5a56dfb4e4b0a300f905c57b) ,” Huff Post, Jan. 11, 2018. Susan Hennessey and Benjamin Wittes, “Congressional Republicans have pulled a bait-and-switch in the Trump-Russia investigation (http://www.chicagotribune.com/news/sns-wp-house-intel-analysis-9d94cdf4-e73a-11e7-a65d-1ac0fd7f097e-20171222-story.html) ," Chicago Tribune, Dec. 22, 2017. clean_body: Republicans want to end the investigation into Trump’s Russia ties. We cannot let them. () The petition to congressional leadership reads: “The congressional investigation into Trump cannot conclude until Special Prosecutor Mueller has wrapped up his investigation.” Add your name: () Dear Activist, () Republicans have sought every chance to delegitimize special counsel Robert Mueller. Now, they are ramping up their attempts to derail Mueller’s inquiry into Trump’s Russia ties with calls to shut down the parallel, ongoing congressional investigations, thus making it appear as if Mueller is on a wild goose chase.1 Republicans have spent months stymying the investigation by refusing to schedule interviews with key witnesses and sitting on requests from Democrats to subpoena relevant documents. Now powerful House Republican leaders such as Rep. Trey Gowdy – an extreme right-wing Republican ideologue – are reportedly talking about shutting down the investigation.2 This is unacceptable. Trump’s lapdogs in Congress are making up excuses to conclude their investigation into Trump’s Russia ties to pressure Mueller to end his investigation – and shut down Democrats like Rep. Adam Schiff who have relentlessly pursued the truth. We need a large outcry right now to shame Republicans into continuing their investigation. Tell congressional leaders: Don’t bow to Trump by ending the Russia investigation. Click here to sign the petition. () There is no way congressional investigators have actually finished their work. No one yet knows the full extent of Trump’s ties to Russia, and special counsel Mueller keeps uncovering new information that warrants congressional investigation. Republicans are sabotaging an investigation with national security implications. House Intelligence Committee Chair Devin Nunes repeatedly refused requests from his Democratic colleagues for interviews with as many as 30 witnesses and blocked potential subpoenas of relevant documents.3 Financial crimes are notoriously difficult to uncover, and thorough investigations can take years.4 It is evident the investigation to date has not been sufficiently thorough. Democratic Congressman Jim Himes says that there are so many unanswered questions that Democrats would have to list them in a minority report if Republicans get their way. 5 Mueller and the congressional investigators have two different jobs. Mueller’s narrow focus is on whether anyone connected to the Trump organization – or Trump himself – committed specific crimes by coordinating with Russians. Congress is investigating Russia’s attempt to hijack our democracy and whether Trump or his campaign engaged in shameful and improper collusion with Russia even if it does not violate specific laws. Congress’ responsibility is to ensure thorough fact finding to uncover answers for the American public. Republicans are refusing to get the real facts in a bid to undermine Robert Mueller, and we cannot let that happen. Tell congressional leaders: Don’t bow to Trump by ending the Russia investigation. Click here to sign the petition. () The effort to derail Mueller began among fringe supporters and the alt-right fake news, but Trump Republicans are now blatantly launching a systematic and coordinated attack on his investigation. The current Republican attempts to end the ongoing congressional investigations are part of an alliance with the extreme right-wing and Trump sycophants in Congress to systematically undermine Mueller and his investigation.6 By insisting that there are no other questions to ask, or evidence to examine, Republicans hope to create a media firestorm from the extreme right wing that will either shut down or sabotage Mueller’s investigation. The public has a right to know the extent of the potential collusion between Trump and his associates and the Kremlin. As long as there are witnesses to interview, and questions remain, Congress must keep up this crucial work. Tell congressional leaders: Don’t bow to Trump by ending Russia investigation. Click the link below to sign the petition. name: Megan West, My Faith Votes
subject: How many?
body:
Hello Peter,
Can I let you in on a little secret? I’m really nervous for tomorrow!
It’s the first time I’ll be participating in the 45th annual March for Life in Washington D.C. I’m nervous because I’ve never participated in a march before. And, I’ll be making my mark in history by marching as an adoptive mom.
But, I’m so excited and deeply humbled to be able to stand with thousands of other citizens who care about the sanctity of life like I do.
Take a guess: For every newborn baby placed for adoption, how many couples are on a waiting list?
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fadoption-waiting-list%2F%3Fa%3D1&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180118qna01&n=1)

[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fadoption-waiting-list%2F%3Fa%3D3&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180118qna01&n=3)
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fadoption-waiting-list%2F%3Fa%3D4&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180118qna01&n=4)
Blessings,
-Megan West

name: Russ Feingold, LegitAction
subject: An appointment we can’t accept
body:
[ Legit ]LegitAction

Friend,

The Trump administration wants a proponent of gerrymandering to take
charge of the Census Bureau. They’re openly attempting to corrupt one
of our oldest institutions to manipulate the census so they can
undermine voting rights enforcement and fair electoral maps.

Thousands of Legit supporters have already signed onto a petition to
say: an opponent of voting rights like Thomas Brunell has no place at
the U.S. Census Bureau. [ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/oppose_brunell_nomination/?t=1002&akid=553%2E356062%2EXOF7Eq ]Will you add your name?

With so many threats to our democratic legitimacy angling for our
attention, the administration would love Brunell’s appointment to slip
through quietly so he can start exploiting the census to the advantage
of his Republican allies. We won’t let that happen.

[ https://act.legitaction.com/sign/oppose_brunell_nomination/?t=1003&akid=553%2E356062%2EXOF7Eq ]Add your name to LegitAction’s petition today. Oppose Thomas
Brunell’s appointment as deputy director of the U.S. Census Bureau.

The louder our resistance, the more effectively we strip Brunell’s
appointment of any shred of false legitimacy it may have.

Thank you for standing up for democratic legitimacy today,

Russ Feingold
LegitAction

name: R. Jai Gillum
subject: Hey, do you trust me?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31ea/9e00690/4313a07c/2403e2bc/1781702042/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/?utm_medium=email&utm_source=gillum&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=em18_180118_WomenM_fnd_gq_act__RJG&source=em18_180118_WomenM_fnd_gq_act__RJG")
Hello,

When it comes to women's issues like health care access, equal pay for equal work, harassment and violence, it boils down to trust -- do our leaders trust that women can make our own choices?

So when Donald Trump was elected, I was worried for the future of my children but especially concerned for my daughter. Thankfully, instead of giving up, millions of Americans stood up, spoke out, and took action to move our country forward.

Hundreds of thousands of women and men took to the streets the day after Trump's inauguration for marches across the nation -- and leaders, like my husband Andrew, used their voices and positions to fight for women's rights.

It's simple: when women succeed, we all succeed. And I think it's about time Florida had a Governor that trusted women and fought alongside them, instead of against them.

**[I know Andrew will be that Governor, just as he's been as a Mayor, husband and father. So chip in $5 now and help elect an ally and advocate as Florida's next Governor.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31ea/9e00690/4313a07c/2403e2bf/1781702042/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/andrew-gillum-trump-womens-march?utm_campaign=em18_180118_WomenM_fnd_gq_act__RJG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em18_180118_WomenM_fnd_gq_act__RJG&utm_content=2+-+I+know+Andrew+will+be+that+Governor+just")**

**[Express Donate: $200](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31ea/9e00690/4313a07c/2403e2b3/1781702042/VEsA/p/eyJlM3NnWVhOck5DQjlmUT09IjoiMjAwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/andrew-gillum-trump-womens-march?utm_campaign=em18_180118_WomenM_fnd_gq_act__RJG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em18_180118_WomenM_fnd_gq_act__RJG&express_lane=true&amount=200&utm_content=6+-+Express+Donate++ask4+")**

From Trump attempting to limit access to no-cost birth control for health and wellness to the attacks on women's health care providers, the last year has been a battle, to say the least.

But as they say, the night is always darkest before the dawn -- and this year we can make it bright.

When Andrew is Governor, he'll work to pass statewide protections to ensure all health policies that cover prescriptions provide no-cost contraceptive coverage and push for equal pay legislation that levels the playing field for women in the workplace. Andrew knows that when women succeed, we all succeed.

**[We must continue to stand up, fight back, and make our voices heard at the ballot box. Help elect Andrew, who will use his voice to make it clear that civil rights will not be denied based on gender, by chipping in $5 now.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb31ea/9e00690/4313a07c/2403e2b5/1781702042/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/andrew-gillum-trump-womens-march?utm_campaign=em18_180118_WomenM_fnd_gq_act__RJG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em18_180118_WomenM_fnd_gq_act__RJG&utm_content=8+-+We+must+continue+to+stand+up+fight+back+")**

R. Jai Gillum

disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
UPDATE: The Hill is reporting that Democrats only need one more GOP Senator to join them in saving net neutrality. Urge the GOP to act now >>
---
[](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d7aa/2b07114c/b8fff927/731ba0eb/1162263039/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/save-net-neutrality?source=PT-20180118-AddYourNameDS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")
**_The Hill: "Democrats are just one GOP vote shy of the 51-vote threshold"_
[Add your name: Tell the GOP to protect net neutrality >>](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d7aa/2b07114c/b8fff927/731ba0eb/1162263039/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/save-net-neutrality?source=PT-20180118-AddYourNameDS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")**
The internet as we know it is on the line, team.

Trump's FCC gutted net neutrality rules and **we only have 25 days to act** before internet companies can slow down your internet or block sites that don't pay them extra.

Under the new rules, your emails could load more slowly, Netflix could take forever to load, and your favorite sites could be blocked entirely -- unless you pay internet providers extra.

But thankfully, Senate Democrats have been working hard to overturn the FCC decision -- and we're making progress.

**Now,_The Hill_ is reporting that with ALL Democrats and one Republican on board, we only need one more GOP Senator to overrule the FCC and ensure a free and fair internet.**

[**Add your name to the growing list of Americans who support net neutrality and stand with Democrats trying to convince Republicans we need a free and open internet.**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d7aa/2b07114c/b8fff927/731ba0eb/1162263039/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/save-net-neutrality?source=PT-20180118-AddYourNameDS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Here is why we need your help right away: Democrats already have the support needed to force Mitch McConnell to allow a vote.

We only have 60 days from the FCC's decision to overrule the FCC and preserve net neutrality.

One month has already passed. And Democrats just need to convince one more GOP Senator to get on board.

**Time is running out. Please add your name to stand with Democrats working to convince Republicans to join them in saving the internet as we know it:**

[**http://www.dscc.org/save-net-neutrality-now**](http://action.dscc.org/page/m/3954d7aa/2b07114c/b8fff927/731ba0eb/1162263039/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVkdTVkpUVkU1QlRVVWxKUT09IjoiIn0=/ "http://action.dscc.org/page/s/save-net-neutrality?source=PT-20180118-AddYourNameDS-ProsHV&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=&firstname=")

Thank you,

Team DSCC

| | **Join Us This Weekend for the National Day of Training**
---
|
---
| This weekend marks a milestone \- it's now been **one year** **since President Donald Trump took the Oath of Office to become our 45th president of the United States!**

While national media and liberal Democrats will spend the weekend repeating the same old tired complaints against President Trump and our Republican leaders in Washington, here in Indiana we'll be taking part in the **National Day of Training** , a special day designed to help you --- our local party leaders and grassroots volunteers -- build the skills needed to share our positive Republican message.

Our **National Day of Training** events are across Indiana, and will provide you with the information and technology you need to make an even greater impact in your community:
**Delaware County Training**
Sunday, Jan. 21
Ball State Student Center
2000 West University Avenue, Muncie
[SIGN UP HERE](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a523a91695&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**1st Congressional District Training**
Saturday, Jan. 20 Twelve Islands
114 S Main Street #1, Crown Point
[SIGN UP HERE](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=99981100e8&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Hamilton County Training**
Saturday, Jan. 20
136 S 9th St, Noblesville
[SIGN UP HERE](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5931085efb&e=1acd9bb3d6)

[SIGN UP HERE](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=fa409bc2a9&e=1acd9bb3d6)
**Vanderburgh County Training**

100 Walnut St., Evansville
[SIGN UP HERE](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=52deae10bc&e=1acd9bb3d6)

**Wells County Phone Bank**
Saturday, Jan. 20
Wells County Republican Headquarters
125 S. Johnson St., Bluffton
[SIGN UP HERE](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a68d82420e&e=1acd9bb3d6)
Sign up NOW for our **National Day of Training** events across Indiana this weekend, and we'll provide you with an overview of the [**Republican Leadership Initiative**](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=a069954ac6&e=1acd9bb3d6), a program that is mobilizing Hoosiers across the state with the leadership skills needed -- such building a successful volunteer network in your community -- to deliver more victories in 2018 and beyond. name: Joe (personal)
subject: I don't work for them, Peter.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Just a few months ago, President Trump promised to come to Indiana and "campaign against me like you wouldn't believe" if I didn't go along with Mitch McConnell's tax plan. But I'm Indiana's hired hel |
---

| | Peter,

Just a few months ago, President Trump promised to come to Indiana and "campaign against me like you wouldn't believe" if I didn't go along with Mitch McConnell's tax plan. But I'm Indiana's hired help in the Senate. I don't work for them -- I work for Hoosiers.

As you know, I voted against the McConnell tax bill that helped his special interest allies instead of working Hoosier families. I'm not afraid to fight back against anyone without Hoosier common sense and the belief that everyone deserves access to health care, a good education, and a fair wage that you can raise a family on. That's exactly why I stood against Mitch McConnell on taxes, and why I'm standing up against attacks on Social Security and Medicare, too.

But to stay in the Senate fighting for you, I'll need your help. We've got a big deadline tonight and we're almost there. **Can you chip in $10 at the link below to help us hit it?**

Here's a link to donate:

**[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-jan18mmnd](http://action.joeforindiana.com/page/m/7f7c2221/7377f838/32e8bfe6/51fa0609/334097677/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-jan18mmnd?refcode=20180118nd&amount=10")**

Thank you,

Joe |

But if we stand together now and give Democratic women our full support, we can send a record number of women to the Senate and take back the majority in 2018.

name: Senator Maggie Hassan
subject: My vote on the funding bill
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hello,
I want you to hear it from me.
I will join my colleagues -- both Republicans and Democrats -- in voting 'no' on the four-week funding bill.
We are within reach of a bipartisan agreement to substantially increase funding to combat the opioid crisis and ensure stability for our budget, while addressing funding for the Children's Health Insurance Program (CHIP), protecting DREAMers, and providing critical funding for community health centers.
Now is not the time to kick this can down the road. Now is the time for both parties to work across the aisle, and it's time for President Trump to stop blocking bipartisan efforts to reach a much-needed deal.
[**If you agree, I need you to stand with me and my colleagues from both parties and send a message to the President: Millions of families are depending on us to do our jobs and reach bipartisan consensus. Please, add your name.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fdffc/2a380d31/67e620f/f1dfb5/3967958940/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjAzMjY0In0=/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/s/20180118_FundingBill?source=20180118_FundingBill_act&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=03264")
Thank you for speaking out.
With every good wish,
Senator Maggie Hassan
